title: How are upcoming (breaking) changes to the Monday SDK/API communicated to developers?
excerpt:
I have seen the changelog pages for the API and App Framework, and this is useful information, but I am not finding information on how Monday communicates upcoming changes that may break a developer app or integration. 
I…

posts.author: SecretSauce (Jeff Helman)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have seen the changelog pages for the API and App Framework, and this is useful information, but I am not finding information on how Monday communicates upcoming changes that may break a developer app or integration.

I get that it is up to developers to determine if an upcoming change will break our app, but waiting until the changelog is published is too late.

Thanks in advance for pointing me to the correct resource for this.

posts.author: rachelatmonday (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @SecretSauce,

All breaking and non-breaking changes are communicated in our changelogs - those are our source of truth!

Our API is versioned and tends to change more frequently. If a version has breaking changes but you need time to migrate, simply pass the API-Version header in your calls and request an older one

Best,
Rachel

posts.author: SecretSauce (Jeff Helman)
posts.cookedHtml: Many thanks. That is the clarity I was seeking.

posts.author: rachelatmonday (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml: Happy to help! Let us know if you have any other questions
